description: A designed ICE VOICE outreach/PSA graphic in portrait format. The right two-thirds is a warm color photograph of two pairs of clasped hands — one person comfortingly holding another's hand with both of theirs, suggesting support/consolation; a seated person in light blue is partially visible. A vertical translucent blue panel overlays the left third carrying the message in white sans-serif text: 'If you’re the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we’re here to' followed by the large bold word 'HELP'. An orange diagonal accent shape cuts across the lower right. A white footer bar at the bottom contains the contact line in blue and orange: '1-855-48-VOICE' and 'ICE.GOV/VOICE', alongside the blue 'V' VOICE logo and the program name 'VOICE — VICTIMS OF IMMIGRATION CRIME ENGAGEMENT'. The visual style is soft and empathetic (warm photo, supportive imagery, blue/orange brand palette) — distinct from ICE's harsher enforcement graphics. Messaging technique: presents ICE as a compassionate victim-services provider while reinforcing the political frame of 'crime linked to immigration.' This appears to be a recurring/reused VOICE template (an essentially identical design appears elsewhere in the dataset). Target audience: crime victims and the public.
notable_text: If you’re the victim of a crime linked to immigration, we’re here to HELP; 1-855-48-VOICE; ICE.GOV/VOICE; VOICE — Victims of Immigration Crime Engagement
